[object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revi.title: IIP base year revised from 2011-12 → 2022-23.
[object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revi.text: IIP base year revised from 2011-12 → 2022-23. [object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revised-from-2011-12-2022-23 IIP base year GDP vs IIP base year economy The number of items changed to 463 from 407 new addition: CCTV, vaccine, aircraft parts, water supply, and waste management. removed: kerosene, sewing machine, CFL Industrial growth Economic indicators Base year revision Manufacturing sector GDP/IIP methodology Manufacturing industry Why is the base year revised? Which products/sectors were added or removed? Difference between GDP and IIP. Important for prelims statement-based questions. how is IIP calculated? Manufacturing still has the highest weight in IIP. IIP (Index of Industrial Production) — SummaryMeasures monthly industrial growth in India. Released by the National Statistical Office. Current base year: 2011–12. Sectors in IIPMnemonic: MME Mining Manufacturing Electricity WeightageManufacturing → highest weight (~77.6%) Mining → ~14.4% Electricity → ~8% FormulaIIP = \frac{Current\ Production}{Base\ Year\ Production}\times100 MeaningIIP > 100 → industrial production increased compared to base year. Higher IIP = industrial expansion. Lower IIP = slowdown. ImportanceUsed to track: industrial growth, economic activity, business cycle, manufacturing performance. UPSC AngleImportant for: GS3 Economy GDP vs IIP Industrial slowdown Manufacturing growth Core sector analysis Relevant economy PYQs repeatedly focus on industrial growth and economic trends.

2026-06-03-wholesale-price-index-base-ye.title: Wholesale Price Index base year revised from 2011–12 → 2022–23.
2026-06-03-wholesale-price-index-base-ye.text: Wholesale Price Index base year revised from 2011–12 → 2022–23. 2026-06-03-wholesale-price-index-base-year-revised-from-201 gs3 WPI base year base year economy India is revising the WPI base year from 2011–12 to 2022–23 and gradually transitioning toward Producer Price Index (PPI) to modernize inflation measurement and align with global standards. WPI basket expanded: 697 → 957 items Weight structure broadly unchanged: Manufactured products still have highest weight (~63%). Government will experimentally introduce: Producer Price Index (PPI) After around 5 years, WPI may be replaced by PPI. Services PPI will also be introduced for: banking, telecom, insurance, railways etc. WPI is released monthly. Reserve Bank of India mainly uses CPI for inflation targeting. Inflation Indices in IndiaCPI → Consumer inflation WPI → Wholesale inflation PPI → Producer inflation Difference between WPI & PPI WPIPPIWholesale pricesProducer pricesIncludes taxes/trade distortionsCleaner producer costMainly goodsGoods + servicesOlder methodologyInternational standard Why shift to PPI?Better GDP deflator More accurate inflation measurement Globally accepted methodology Better reflects producer costs
